title: luma verticillaris
intro: Luma verticillaris, also known as the Chilean cinnamon or Chilean laurel, is a flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Luma verticillaris include reducing fever, relieving pain, and acting as an antioxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. Luma verticillaris is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications, such as salves and creams, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
